name: C/p Brownie Cakes in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease two 1-pint straight-sided wide-mouth canning jars and line the bottom of each jar with waxed paper. Set aside.
2. In a small bowl, mix together flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (if desired). Set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine margarine, water, and cocoa powder; heat and stir until the margarine is melted and the mixture is well blended. Remove from heat and stir in the flour mixture. Add buttermilk, egg, and vanilla; beat by hand until smooth. Stir in nuts.
4. Pour the mixture into the prepared canning jars. Cover the jars tightly with greased foil (grease-side down). Place the jars in a 3½ to 6-quart crockery cooker with liner in place. Cover.
5. Cook on high heat setting for 2¼ to 3 hours, or until cakes spring back when touched and a wooden toothpick inserted near the centers comes out clean. Remove jars from cooker; cool about 10 minutes. Unmold cakes; remove waxed paper. Serve warm or cool with ice cream. Makes 8 servings.
WARNING: Use only CANNING JARS for this recipe. Others may not be tempered to withstand the heat. Do NOT use coffee or vegetable cans as most contain lead and are painted or sealed with materials that may give off toxic gases when heated.
